pair_a.prompt: I'm going to show you two short descriptions of laptop ads. Read both, then tell me which one feels more like a brand you'd take seriously — and why.

ad_a.label: Dry / witty challenger
ad_a.description: A minimalist ad. No music. A single sentence: 'It's a laptop. Not a lifestyle.' Below: a spec sheet, a three-year warranty, and a price that undercuts the competition by 30%.

ad_b.label: Earnest / aspirational hero
ad_b.description: A montage of people creating and collaborating — a writer on a train, a designer in a cafe, a video editor in an apartment. Uplifting score. Final line: 'Built for the people who build the future.'

pair_b.prompt: Here's a second pair. Same instructions — which feels more like a brand you'd take seriously, and why.

ad_a.label: Serious / authoritative
ad_a.description: A laptop sits on a desk in a quiet corporate office. Calm voice-over lists security features, enterprise support hours, and a five-year roadmap. Tagline: 'Trusted by 47 of the Fortune 100.'

ad_b.label: Playful / irreverent peer
ad_b.description: Fast cuts. A developer throws her laptop into a bag, drops it twice, opens it at a rooftop bar, keeps coding. Text on screen: 'Relax. So is it.' No voice-over.

prosumer.projective_close: A colleague at another company asks you whether they should buy a Lenovo or a Dell for their own work. What do you tell them?

pair_a.prompt: I'm going to show you two short laptop ads. Read both, then tell me which one feels more like a brand you'd take seriously for your fleet — and why.

ad_a.label: Pragmatic reliability
ad_a.description: A small-business owner talks directly to camera: 'Last year, I lost three days of revenue to a laptop failure. This year, I'm not.' Cut to product, three-year onsite warranty callout, and a price. No music.

ad_b.label: Partnership narrative
ad_b.description: A reseller and a client CFO shake hands. Voice-over: 'We don't just sell you laptops. We build the fleet that builds your business.' Warm color grading, soft piano.

pair_b.prompt: Here's a second pair. Same question — which feels more like a brand you'd take seriously, and why.

ad_a.label: Operator-focused
ad_a.description: A fast-cut montage of IT-admin workflows — imaging, zero-touch deployment, single-pane-of-glass management dashboard. Tagline: 'For the person everyone calls when it breaks.'

ad_b.label: Employee-pull
ad_b.description: Employees unboxing laptops, smiling, getting to work. Focus on the end-user experience. Tagline: 'Your team will thank you.'

smb_it.projective_close: A peer who runs IT at another small company is about to make their next laptop purchase. They ask you what they should buy and why. What do you tell them?

pair_a.prompt: I'm going to show you two short laptop ads. Read both, then tell me which one feels more like a brand you'd actually buy — and why.

ad_a.label: Aspirational lifestyle
ad_a.description: A person opens a sleek laptop on a cafe table. Cut to travel, photography, writing, coffee, plants. Warm music. Tagline: 'Make room for the life you want.'

ad_b.label: Value-forward
ad_b.description: Plain white background. Laptop rotating. Text appears: 'All the things you actually need. None of the ones you don't. $799.' No music.

pair_b.prompt: Here's a second pair. Same question — which one feels more like a brand you'd actually buy, and why.

ad_a.label: Family / practical
ad_a.description: A mom hands a laptop to her teen for school. Cut to homework, video calls, a kitchen scene. Voice-over: 'Built for real life.' Gentle music.

ad_b.label: Playful / self-expression
ad_b.description: Fast cuts. Colors. A creator, a gamer, a student, a grandparent on a video call — all using laptops in very different ways. Tagline: 'However you use it, it's yours.'

consumer.projective_close: A friend is about to buy their first laptop in five years. They ask you what they should get. What do you tell them, and why?
